2.4.49.description: Path traversal vulnerability allowing remote file access.
hardening: Restrict filesystem access using proper Allow/Deny rules.

2.4.50.description: Remote code execution via path traversal.

hardening: Hide Apache version using 'ServerTokens Prod' and 'ServerSignature Off'.
Remove X-Powered-By header to reduce fingerprinting.

tls_1_0.description: Outdated TLS protocol with known weaknesses.
hardening: Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 in Apache SSL configuration.

directory_listing.description: Directory listing enabled may expose sensitive files.

1.18.0.description: Older stable release lacking modern TLS defaults.
hardening: Upgrade to the latest stable Nginx release.
Enable modern TLS ciphers and disable outdated protocols.
Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 in Nginx SSL configuration.

7.2.description: Older OpenSSH version with known weaknesses.
hardening: Upgrade OpenSSH to a modern version.
Disable weak ciphers and MAC algorithms.

password_auth_enabled.description: Password authentication is insecure and vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

permit_root_login.description: Root login over SSH increases attack surface.

1.description: SMBv1 is deprecated and vulnerable to multiple remote code execution attacks.
hardening: Restrict SMB access to internal networks only.

2.description: SMBv2 lacks modern security features found in SMBv3.

anonymous_access.description: Anonymous SMB access exposes sensitive shares.

hardening: Replace FTP with SFTP or FTPS.

anonymous_login.description: Anonymous FTP login may expose sensitive files.
hardening: Disable anonymous FTP unless absolutely required.
Restrict access to read-only public directories.

nla_disabled.description: Network Level Authentication (NLA) disabled increases RDP attack surface.
hardening: Enable NLA for all RDP connections.
Restrict RDP access to VPN or jump hosts.

weak_encryption.description: RDP encryption not set to High or FIPS.
hardening: Enable High or FIPS-compliant RDP encryption.

open_resolver.description: Open DNS resolvers can be abused for amplification attacks.
hardening: Restrict DNS queries to trusted networks.

zone_transfer_enabled.description: Unrestricted DNS zone transfers expose domain structure.
hardening: Restrict AXFR zone transfers to authorized secondary DNS servers.

missing_spf.description: SPF record missing increases spoofing risk.
hardening: Add a valid SPF record to DNS.

missing_dkim.description: DKIM not configured reduces email integrity.
hardening: Enable DKIM signing for outbound email.

missing_dmarc.description: DMARC missing allows spoofing and phishing.
hardening: Add a DMARC policy (p=quarantine or p=reject).

1.description: SNMPv1 uses no encryption and weak authentication.
hardening: Use SNMPv3 with authentication and encryption.

public_community.description: Default SNMP community string 'public' is widely known.
hardening: Restrict SNMP access to management networks.

rc4.description: RC4 cipher is broken and insecure.
hardening: Remove RC4 from allowed cipher suites.

5.5.description: MySQL 5.5 is end-of-life and lacks modern security patches.
hardening: Require strong authentication for all accounts.
Use dedicated admin accounts with limited privileges.

9.3.description: PostgreSQL 9.3 is end-of-life and missing security updates.
hardening: Restrict access to trusted networks only.
Restrict pg_hba.conf to specific IP ranges.

no_auth.description: Redis authentication disabled; server may be exposed.
hardening: Bind Redis to localhost or internal interfaces only.
Use firewall rules to restrict access.

protected_mode_off.description: Redis protected mode disabled; dangerous when exposed.
hardening: Ensure Redis is not exposed to the public internet.

auth_disabled.description: MongoDB authentication disabled; full database exposure possible.
hardening: Bind MongoDB to localhost or internal interfaces only.

enabled.description: Telnet transmits all data in plaintext and should never be exposed.
hardening: Replace with SSH for secure remote access.

server_2008.description: Windows Server 2008 is end-of-life and vulnerable.
hardening: Upgrade to a supported Windows Server version.
Disable SMBv1 and enforce modern authentication.
Plan upgrade to Server 2019 or later.
Migrate to Rocky Linux, AlmaLinux, or CentOS Stream.
Harden SSH and disable legacy services.

public_exposure.description: Service exposed to the public internet.
hardening: Restrict service exposure using firewall rules.
Place sensitive services behind VPN or jump hosts.
